focus: Lessons making it worse, lost driver ability, draw bias skepticism

PAIN.quote: My game has gone down hill this season, almost makes me want to quit
when I leave and hit the course (sparingly) this year, it's like I never played before. Slicing, massive loss in distance. I am beyond confused as well as everyone I play with
PAIN.context: Lesson gains not transferring to course

PAIN.quote: I can totally relate to that. Years ago before I quit the game I took a couple of lessons to try and improve my distance and accuracy. Nothing at all against the instructor - he was extremely knowledgeable, but he essentially tore down and re-engineered my swing into something that worked for like a day
I know it goes against conventional wisdom around here but my experience is what it is... lessons did more harm than good for me
Getting worse after a lesson is very common, especially if you have decent hand eye coordination and you made sense out of chaos. Those swings fade quickly with age
PAIN.context: Getting worse after lessons is common

PAIN.quote: 9 times out of 10 when I hit my driver the ball viciously slices at almost a horizontal angle (comes off the tee straight, then does like a 90 degree turn)
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Can't fix my driver slice any advice

PAIN.quote: I've tried tee'ing the ball at different heights but It seems like even 'tee it high and let it fly' doesn't help me
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Can't fix my driver slice any advice

PAIN.quote: I'll have a few good shots and then get cocky and on the next tee I pull out the driver and sort of think, 'well what could really go wrong?' and then a whole lot goes wrong and my score goes in the gutter
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Can't fix my driver slice any advice

PAIN.quote: I have officially lost the ability to hit my driver. Back in November/December I was driving the ball damn well, to the point where I stepped up and expected to hit the ball 250ish in the fairway. Fast forward to this past weekend, and good f'ing god it's a mess
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - I have officially lost the ability to hit my driver

PAIN.quote: Missed every single fairway, miss has gone from a hook to a banana slice (which I thought I'd fixed 5 months ago). It's seriously ruining rounds, as I'm playing my second shot out of the trees every time
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - I have officially lost the ability to hit my driver

PAIN.quote: I feel your pain. I cycle through hitting my driver great and hitting it so bad that I leave it at home
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - I have officially lost the ability to hit my driver
PAIN.context: Driver left at home due to inconsistency

PAIN.quote: I shared the same pain where I lost my ability to hit my driver last October after a short holiday break. I was playing the best golf before that. My slice lasted me for 4 months
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - I have officially lost the ability to hit my driver

PAIN.quote: I hear all the time - on here, from my friends, from random people I play with - that someone is working on changing their swing. When I start asking questions about how they are going about it, I can see that it is more a hope and a prayer more than an actual plan
PAIN.source: MyGolfSpy - Draw biased drivers vs working on swing
PAIN.context: Most swing work is wishful thinking

PAIN.quote: Swing changes are HARD and take time. Most amateurs do not have the time required to drastically change their swing
PAIN.source: MyGolfSpy - Draw biased drivers vs working on swing
PAIN.context: Reality check on swing change difficulty

PAIN.quote: my miss with driver is two way and tends to change as the round goes on, right around the turn
PAIN.source: MyGolfSpy - Draw biased drivers vs working on swing

HOPE.quote: Slow the fock down and swing within myself. When I slow down it allows time for my body parts to get to the right positions at the right times. And, I can actually control the club face and work the ball. And (of course), I hit it farther
HOPE.source: TheSandTrap - I have officially lost the ability to hit my driver

HOPE.quote: the harder you hit it the shorter it goes paradox
HOPE.source: TheSandTrap - I have officially lost the ability to hit my driver

HOPE.quote: I stopped slicing by really focusing on an inside out swing. I did this by bringing the club down very slowly and making sure the end of the grip pointed at the ball
HOPE.source: TheSandTrap - Can't fix my driver slice any advice

HOPE.quote: With 5 lessons (first time ever getting lessons) I'm hitting the ball the straightest ever, and more consistently too. The coaching has made a huge difference for me to the point where my swing actually feels comfortable
HOPE.source: MyGolfSpy - Draw biased drivers vs working on swing

HOPE.quote: Having a club that fits your swing can really help both in the improvement and enjoyment of the game
HOPE.source: MyGolfSpy - Draw biased drivers vs working on swing

HOPE.quote: Try hitting the 3 wood for a few weeks off of the tee and leave the driver at home until you develop some confidence. Then bring out the driver, and mentally tell yourself that you have a 3 wood
HOPE.source: TheSandTrap - I have officially lost the ability to hit my driver

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: There are a million articles, videos, tips, etc. on 'how to cure your slice', and almost all of them talk about either: closing the clubface or swinging in to out. The problem though is it's one thing to think in to out and another to actually execute
ROOT_CAUSE.source: TheSandTrap - Can't fix my driver slice any advice

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: The slice is being produced by coming across the ball from outside->in (over the top)
ROOT_CAUSE.source: TheSandTrap - Can't fix my driver slice any advice
ROOT_CAUSE.context: Over the top swing path diagnosis

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: It seems that when I am driving it well, I start cranking it up unnoticeably over time until I start mishitting it a little, which will cause some crookedness and loss of distance, which leads to my trying to hit it harder
ROOT_CAUSE.source: TheSandTrap - I have officially lost the ability to hit my driver

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: It's not the driver that will fix any problem, it's the swing path or an open/closed face at impact
ROOT_CAUSE.source: MyGolfSpy - Draw biased drivers vs working on swing
ROOT_CAUSE.context: Equipment can't fix fundamental swing issues

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: most golfers have a swing that results in a ball flight that goes left to right for rh golfers. The draw bias driver isn't going to make them start automatically hitting the ball right to left. It's going to help minimize the amount
ROOT_CAUSE.source: MyGolfSpy - Draw biased drivers vs working on swing
ROOT_CAUSE.context: Draw bias limitation - minimizes not fixes

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: trust your instincts and try not to let two million swing thoughts clutter up your mind when you're setting up at address
Super tough to change a swing pattern. I think most golfers want an instructor to say, 'put two tees in your left pocket and you'll bomb it down the middle every time.' If only it were that easy
I would also suggest bagging the driver until you get it worked out on the range. No need to put yourself in a bad spot off the tee during the round
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: TheSandTrap - Can't fix my driver slice any advice

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: focus on turning my chest away from the ball. Make a full shoulder turn - getting your back facing to the target at the top of your backswing
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: TheSandTrap - Can't fix my driver slice any advice

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: Setup for a draw. Address the club behind the ball aiming at the target, now address your feet aiming slightly right of target - this will help you get back at the ball from inside out
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: TheSandTrap - Can't fix my driver slice any advice

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: If you are TRULY going to be working on changing your swing, then yes - I would say your coach is right. But be honest with yourself. If changing your swing is just watching a bunch of youtube videos, going to the range a few times and hoping to see results, then no, you shouldn't stay away from draw biased driver
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: MyGolfSpy - Draw biased drivers vs working on swing

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: A few years back I bought an adjustable driver - GBB, and thought by adjusting the sliding weight it would help my slice. Nope - slice was just as bad
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: MyGolfSpy - Draw biased drivers vs working on swing

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: Today I game a 410SFT, and generally don't slice but it's because I worked on my swing. My sense is that the swing change made the biggest difference
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: MyGolfSpy - Draw biased drivers vs working on swing
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.context: Ping SFT user credits swing work not club

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: I picked up a Rogue draw bias driver last year. While it seemed to help a bit in keeping shots straighter, I also ripped off some wicked hooks that I have NEVER done before
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: MyGolfSpy - Draw biased drivers vs working on swing
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.context: Draw bias caused new hook problem

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: While a draw biased club may help some people with a fairly decent swing turn a slight fade into a draw, it's not going to magically turn a huge slice into a nice baby draw
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: MyGolfSpy - Draw biased drivers vs working on swing
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.context: Draw bias limitation - only helps slight fade

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: The problem with today's society is that we all look for the QUICK FIX. We want to blame something else instead of ourselves. Look, you are a golfer, golf is hard and takes a long time to get good
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: MyGolfSpy - Draw biased drivers vs working on swing

VILLAIN.quote: We build 'quick fixes' to compensate for the bigger holes in our swings. These fixes can keep our scores down from round to round, but they limit us from long-term improvement

There are some core issues in most players' swings that they will likely fight their entire golfing life. I fire my hips open way too fast and too early in the downswing. I work on drills to not do that constantly and, yet, it pops up every couple of weeks
VILLAIN.context: Core swing faults never fully fixed

VILLAIN.quote: Nine times out of ten it's not the club
VILLAIN.source: TheSandTrap - I have officially lost the ability to hit my driver

VILLAIN.quote: I see nothing wrong with draw biased clubs being used to help a minor issue, but don't expect such adjustments to fix fundamentally bad swings
VILLAIN.source: MyGolfSpy - Draw biased drivers vs working on swing
VILLAIN.context: Draw bias only for minor issues

sources_covered: TheSandTrap - Can't fix my driver slice any advice
TheSandTrap - I have officially lost the ability to hit my driver
MyGolfSpy - Draw biased drivers vs working on swing
high_value_themes_identified: Lessons often make things worse initially
Getting worse after lessons is VERY common
Draw bias can create NEW hook problems
Slowing down swing = more distance paradox
Most amateur swing work is 'hope and prayer'
